title: gohai
description: Canonical schema for gohai host inventory output. Describes the full Facts structure returned by gohai.New().Collect(), covering system identity, hardware, network, cloud provider metadata, virtualization, security, software, users, and miscellaneous host facts.

gohai_version.description: gohai binary/library version that produced this output.

collected_at.description: Unix epoch seconds when collection completed.

collect_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the collection run completed.

collect_duration_ns.description: Total wall-clock duration of the collection run in nanoseconds.

Timings.description: Per-collector runtime observability data, populated when WithTimings() is passed to gohai.New.

collectors.description: Map of collector name to its timing entry.

CollectorTiming.description: One collector's per-run timing and status entry.

duration_ns.description: Wall-clock duration of this collector's run in nanoseconds.

status.description: Outcome of the collector run: "ok" for success, "err" for failure.

error.description: Error message when the collector failed. Absent on success.

Platform.description: OS identification: canonical distro/product name, version, family, architecture, and build identifier.

name.description: Canonical distro or product name after Ohai-style remap: "ubuntu", "redhat", "darwin", "amazon".

version.description: OS version string: "24.04" for Ubuntu, "14.4.1" for macOS.

version_extra.description: Extra version info, such as macOS Rapid Security Response patches.

build.description: OS build identifier. macOS BuildVersion (e.g. "23F79"); empty on Linux.

Hostname.description: Host naming: short hostname, FQDN via DNS canonicalization, domain portion, and raw machine name.

machine_name.description: Raw `hostname` output. On macOS this is the ComputerName-derived friendly name.

fqdn.description: Fully qualified domain name resolved via forward+reverse DNS (e.g. "web01.example.com").

domain.description: Domain portion of the FQDN (e.g. "example.com"). Empty when DNS canonicalization fails.

Kernel.description: Kernel identity from uname(2): sysname, release, version string, machine architecture, and macOS Rosetta 2 translation state.

release.description: Kernel release string (uname -r): e.g. "5.15.0-47-generic" or "23.4.0". OCSF: os.kernel_release.

version.description: Kernel version/build string (uname -v): full build identifier including date and compiler.

processor.description: Processor type (uname -p synthesis). Same as machine on Linux/Darwin.

os.description: Operating system name (uname -o synthesis): "GNU/Linux" on Linux, "Darwin" on macOS. OCSF: os.type.

rosetta_translated.description: True when the current process is running under Apple Rosetta 2 translation (x86_64 binary on ARM Mac). macOS only.

KernelModules.description: Loaded kernel modules (Linux /proc/modules) or kexts (macOS kextstat). Keyed by module/kext identifier.

modules.description: Map of module name to its metadata. Empty on hosts with no loaded modules.

KernelModule.description: A single loaded kernel module or macOS kext.

refcount.description: Number of instances of this module currently loaded (reference count).

version.description: Module version string. Linux: from /sys/module/<name>/version. macOS: from kextstat.

index.description: Kext load order index (macOS only, from kextstat column 1). Zero on Linux.

Uptime.description: System uptime, boot timestamp, and (Linux only) aggregate CPU idle time.

seconds.description: Seconds elapsed since the system booted.

boot_time.description: Unix epoch timestamp of the most recent boot.

idle_seconds.description: Aggregate CPU idle time in seconds across all cores (Linux only, from /proc/uptime).

Virtualization.description: Hypervisor and container runtime detection. A host can participate in multiple layers simultaneously (e.g. KVM guest running Docker).

system.description: Primary/innermost detected virtualization system: "docker", "kvm", "vmware", "lxc", etc. Empty on bare metal.

role.description: Role in the primary system: "host" (running VMs/containers) or "guest" (running inside a VM/container). Empty on bare metal.

systems.description: Every detected virtualization layer as a map of system name to role. Example: {"kvm": "guest", "docker": "host"}.

hypervisor_host.description: Hostname of the hypervisor, when the guest can detect it (e.g. Hyper-V KVP pool).

MachineID.description: Stable machine identifier that persists across reboots. Sourced from /etc/machine-id (Linux) or IOPlatformUUID (macOS).

id.description: Stable host identifier. Survives reboots; changes on re-provisioning or OS reinstall.

CPU.description: CPU topology, model identification, feature flags, cache layout, NUMA topology, and hardware vulnerability mitigation status.

count.description: Logical CPU count -- threads visible to the OS scheduler. Includes hyperthreads. OCSF: device.cpu_count.

sockets.description: Number of physical CPU packages/sockets installed.

cores.description: Total physical core count across all sockets. OCSF: device.cpu_cores.

model_name.description: Human-readable CPU model string (e.g. "Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10700K", "Apple M1 Pro").

family.description: CPU family number (CPUID). Platform-dependent string representation.

model.description: CPU model number within the family (CPUID).

stepping.description: CPU stepping/revision within the model. Identifies silicon errata level.

mhz.description: Current CPU clock speed in MHz as reported by /proc/cpuinfo or sysctl.

cache_size.description: Aggregate cache size in KB from /proc/cpuinfo (typically L2 or last-level cache).

flags.description: CPU feature flags (e.g. "sse4_2", "avx2", "aes"). From /proc/cpuinfo or sysctl.

caches.description: Per-level cache sizes from lscpu (Linux only). Strings mirror lscpu output verbatim.

numa_nodes.description: NUMA topology: map of node ID (integer key as string) to list of CPU indices assigned to that node. Linux only.

numa_nodes_count.description: Total number of NUMA nodes reported by lscpu. Linux only.

vulnerabilities.description: CPU hardware vulnerability mitigation status from /sys/devices/system/cpu/vulnerabilities/. Map of vulnerability name to status text. Linux only.

cpus_online.description: Number of CPUs currently online (Linux, from lscpu).

cpus_offline.description: Number of CPUs currently offline (Linux, from lscpu).

bios_vendor_id.description: BIOS-reported CPU vendor ID (Linux, from lscpu 'BIOS Vendor ID').

bios_model_name.description: BIOS-reported CPU model name (Linux, from lscpu 'BIOS Model name').

machine_type.description: Machine type identifier, primarily for s390x mainframes (Linux, from lscpu).

mhz_max.description: Maximum CPU frequency in MHz as a string (Linux, from lscpu 'CPU max MHz').

mhz_min.description: Minimum CPU frequency in MHz as a string (Linux, from lscpu 'CPU min MHz').

mhz_dynamic.description: Dynamic CPU frequency in MHz (s390x, from lscpu 'CPU dynamic MHz').

cpu_opmodes.description: CPU operation modes (e.g. ["32-bit", "64-bit"]). Linux, from lscpu.

byte_order.description: CPU byte order: "little endian" or "big endian" (Linux, from lscpu).

address_sizes.description: Physical and virtual address sizes (e.g. ["48 bits physical", "48 bits virtual"]). Linux, from lscpu.

virtualization.description: CPU virtualization capability reported by lscpu (e.g. "VT-x", "AMD-V"). Linux only.

virtualization_type.description: Virtualization type from lscpu (e.g. "full", "para"). Linux only.

hypervisor_vendor.description: Hypervisor vendor detected by lscpu (e.g. "KVM", "Microsoft", "VMware"). Linux only.

dispatching_mode.description: CPU dispatching mode for s390x mainframes (Linux, from lscpu).

cpus.description: Per-logical-CPU breakdown. One entry per processor line in /proc/cpuinfo. Matters on hybrid-core architectures.

CPUCaches.description: Per-level cache sizes from lscpu. Values are verbatim strings (e.g. "32 KiB", "1 MiB").

l2d.description: L2 data cache size (rare, some ARM).

l2i.description: L2 instruction cache size (rare, some ARM).

l4.description: L4 cache size (Intel eDRAM on some Broadwell models).

CPUEntry.description: One logical CPU's identity and topology placement from /proc/cpuinfo.

vendor_id.description: CPU vendor string for this logical CPU.

family.description: CPU family for this logical CPU.

model.description: CPU model for this logical CPU.

model_name.description: Human-readable model name for this logical CPU.

physical_id.description: Socket index this CPU belongs to.

core_id.description: Physical core index within the socket.

cores.description: Number of cores on this CPU's socket.

mhz.description: Current clock speed of this logical CPU in MHz.

cache_size.description: Cache size in KB for this logical CPU.

flags.description: Feature flags for this logical CPU.

Load.description: System load averages representing runnable+uninterruptible task counts averaged over 1, 5, and 15 minute windows.

Memory.description: System memory totals, usage buckets, kernel allocations, page-cache state, hugepages, and swap. Values are in bytes. Linux-only fields stay zero on macOS and vice versa.

total.description: Total physical memory installed in bytes. OCSF: device.memory_size.

available.description: Memory available for new allocations without swapping (MemAvailable on Linux, free+inactive on macOS).

used.description: Memory currently in use (total - available, approximately).

used_percent.description: Percentage of total memory currently in use (0.0-100.0).

free.description: Memory not used for anything (MemFree). Much lower than available on Linux due to page cache.

active.description: Memory recently accessed and not easily reclaimable. Both Linux and macOS.

inactive.description: Memory not recently accessed, candidate for reclaim. Both Linux and macOS.

active_anon.description: Active anonymous memory (process heap/stack). Linux only.

active_file.description: Active file-backed memory (page cache). Linux only.

unevictable.description: Memory that cannot be reclaimed (mlock, ramfs). Linux only.

wired.description: Memory wired down by the kernel, cannot be paged out. macOS only.

speculative.description: Speculatively allocated memory (macOS vm_stat). macOS only.

compressed.description: Memory compressed by the macOS compressor. macOS only.

buffers.description: Memory used for block device I/O buffers. Linux only.

cached.description: Memory used by the page cache (Cached in /proc/meminfo). Linux only.

dirty.description: Memory waiting to be written to disk. Linux only.

writeback.description: Memory actively being written back to disk. Linux only.

writeback_tmp.description: Memory used for FUSE writeback temporary buffers. Linux only.

shared.description: Memory used by tmpfs and shared memory segments (Shmem in /proc/meminfo).

mapped.description: Memory mapped into process address spaces via mmap. Linux only.

slab.description: Total kernel slab allocator memory. Linux only.

s_reclaimable.description: Reclaimable slab memory (dentry/inode caches). Linux only.

page_tables.description: Memory used by page table entries. Linux only.

kernel_stack.description: Memory used for kernel thread stacks. Linux only.

percpu.description: Per-CPU kernel data structures. Linux only.

high_total.description: Total high memory (32-bit kernels with >4GB RAM only). Linux only.

nfs_unstable.description: NFS pages sent to the server but not yet committed. Linux only.

bounce.description: Memory used for block device bounce buffers. Linux only.

anon_pages.description: Anonymous pages mapped into user space. Linux only.

shmem.description: Shared memory and tmpfs usage (Shmem). Linux only.

commit_limit.description: Maximum memory that can be committed (overcommit limit). Linux only.

committed_as.description: Currently committed memory (sum of all process allocations). Linux only.

vmalloc_total.description: Total vmalloc address space. Linux only.

vmalloc_chunk.description: Largest contiguous free vmalloc block. Linux only.

DirectMap.description: Physical memory covered by each page-table mapping granularity. From /proc/meminfo DirectMap entries.

map_4k.description: Physical memory mapped with 4K pages in bytes.

map_2m.description: Physical memory mapped with 2M huge pages in bytes.

map_1g.description: Physical memory mapped with 1G huge pages in bytes.

Hugepages.description: Hugepage configuration and usage from /proc/meminfo. Populated only when the kernel has hugepage support.

total.description: Total number of persistent hugepages configured (HugePages_Total).

free.description: Number of hugepages not yet allocated (HugePages_Free).

reserved.description: Hugepages reserved but not yet faulted (HugePages_Rsvd).

surplus.description: Surplus hugepages above the persistent pool (HugePages_Surp).

size.description: Size of each hugepage in bytes (Hugepagesize).

anon_hugepages.description: Transparent hugepage memory currently in use (AnonHugePages).

hugetlb.description: Total hugepage memory (Hugetlb), including all page sizes.

total.description: Total swap space configured in bytes.

used.description: Swap space currently in use in bytes.

used_percent.description: Percentage of swap space in use (0.0-100.0).

cached.description: Swap pages also present in RAM (SwapCached). Linux only.

Filesystem.description: Mounted and unmounted filesystems. Includes capacity/usage/inode counters for mounts, UUID/label from lsblk, and ZFS datasets when the zfs binary is on PATH.

mounts.description: Active mount table with capacity, usage, and inode counters.

unmounted.description: Block devices with a filesystem but no active mountpoint (LUKS, inactive LVs, etc.). Linux only.

zfs_datasets.description: ZFS datasets with full property sets. Linux only, when zfs binary is on PATH.

FilesystemMount.description: One mounted filesystem with capacity, usage, and inode counters.

uuid.description: Filesystem UUID from lsblk. Linux only.

label.description: Filesystem label from lsblk. Linux only.

part_uuid.description: GPT partition UUID from lsblk. Linux only.

part_label.description: GPT partition label from lsblk. Linux only.

BtrfsInfo.description: Btrfs-specific allocation and RAID data from /sys/fs/btrfs/<uuid>/allocation/. Linux only.

BtrfsAllocation.description: Bytes allocated and used for one btrfs block-group type.

total_bytes.description: Total bytes allocated for this block-group type.

bytes_used.description: Bytes actually used within the allocation.

FilesystemUnmounted.description: A block device with a filesystem that is not currently mounted.

ZFSDataset.description: One ZFS dataset (filesystem, volume, snapshot, or bookmark) with its full property set.

mountpoint.description: Mount path if set to a real path. Empty for snapshots, volumes, or mountpoint=none.

is_pool.description: True when this dataset is a zpool root (no slash in the name).

parents.description: Every proper ancestor dataset path, shallowest first.

properties.description: Full ZFS property set from `zfs get all`. Keys are property names.

ZFSProperty.description: One ZFS property value with its source annotation.

Disk.description: Per-device disk I/O counters (read/write counts, bytes, and timing).

devices.description: List of block devices with their I/O statistics.

DiskDevice.description: I/O counters for a single block device.

read_bytes.description: Total bytes read from the device.

write_bytes.description: Total bytes written to the device.

read_time.description: Total milliseconds spent on read operations. Linux only.

write_time.description: Total milliseconds spent on write operations. Linux only.

io_time.description: Total milliseconds spent doing I/O (weighted). Linux only.

Network.description: Network interfaces with addresses, routes, ARP/NDP neighbours, I/O counters, ethtool data, and default gateway information.

interfaces.description: All network interfaces on the host.

neighbours.description: ARP (IPv4) and NDP (IPv6) neighbour cache entries.

default_interface.description: Name of the interface carrying the IPv4 default route.

default_inet6_interface.description: Name of the interface carrying the IPv6 default route.

NetworkInterface.description: A single network interface with addresses, counters, driver info, and optional ethtool/VLAN/tunnel/XDP metadata.

mac.description: Hardware (MAC) address in colon-separated hex notation. OCSF: network_interface.mac.

speed.description: Link speed string from ghw (e.g. "1000Mb/s"). Linux only.

duplex.description: Link duplex mode: "half", "full", or "unknown". Linux only.

addresses.description: IP addresses bound to this interface.

routes.description: Routes associated with this specific interface.

NetworkAddress.description: One IP address bound to an interface with family, prefix length, and scope.

destination.description: Route destination network in CIDR notation (e.g. "0.0.0.0/0", "10.0.0.0/8").

gateway.description: Next-hop gateway address. Empty for directly connected routes.

interface.description: Egress interface name for this route.

source.description: Preferred source address for this route.

proto.description: Routing protocol that installed this route (e.g. "kernel", "dhcp", "bird").

metric.description: Route metric / priority. Lower values are preferred.

NetworkNeighbour.description: One ARP or NDP cache entry.

interface.description: Interface through which the neighbour is reachable.

state.description: Neighbour state: REACHABLE, STALE, DELAY, PROBE, PERMANENT, NOARP, INCOMPLETE, FAILED.

NetworkCounters.description: I/O counters for one network interface.

NetworkEthtool.description: Per-interface ethtool data sourced from ethtool subcommands. Linux only, Ethernet interfaces only.

driver_info.description: Driver metadata from `ethtool -i` (driver, version, firmware_version, bus_info, etc.).

ring_params.description: Ring buffer parameters from `ethtool -g` (max_rx, current_rx, etc.).

coalesce_params.description: Interrupt coalescing parameters from `ethtool -c` (rx_usecs, tx_max_coalesced_frames, adaptive_rx, etc.). Mixed integer and string values.

offload_params.description: Offload feature toggles from `ethtool -k` (tx-checksum-ipv4: on, generic-receive-offload: off, etc.).

pause_params.description: Flow-control pause parameters from `ethtool -a` (autonegotiate, rx, tx).

NetworkVLAN.description: VLAN sub-interface metadata from `ip -d link`. Linux only.

flags.description: VLAN flags: REORDER_HDR, GVRP, LOOSE_BINDING, etc.

NetworkTunnel.description: IP-in-IP or IP6 tunnel metadata from `ip -d link`. Linux only.

flowlabel.description: IPv6 flow label for tunnel packets.

NetworkXDP.description: Attached XDP (eXpress Data Path) eBPF programs on the interface. Linux only.

Process.description: Snapshot of running processes at collection time.

count.description: Total number of processes captured in this snapshot.

ProcessEntry.description: One running process snapshot. Fields that cannot be read (permission denied, zombie) are left empty.

command_line.description: Full command line with arguments. OCSF: process.cmd_line.

state.description: Process state: R (running), S (sleeping), D (disk sleep), Z (zombie), T (stopped), I (idle).

start_time.description: Process start time as Unix epoch seconds.

Users.description: System user and group account databases from /etc/passwd and /etc/group, plus the effective current user.

passwd.description: Map of username to passwd entry.

group.description: Map of group name to group entry.

current_user.description: Username of the effective current user (matching the EUID in the passwd map).

gecos.description: GECOS field (full name and contact info).

members.description: Usernames that are members of this group.

Sessions.description: Currently logged-in user sessions from loginctl (systemd) or utmp/utmpx.

terminal.description: Terminal device (e.g. "pts/0", "tty1"). May be empty for graphical sessions.

host.description: Remote host address for SSH sessions. Empty for local sessions.

started.description: Session start time as Unix epoch seconds (utmp path only).

uid.description: Numeric user ID as string (loginctl path only).

Timezone.description: System timezone: IANA name, short abbreviation, and UTC offset in seconds.

offset.description: UTC offset in seconds. Negative for west of UTC (e.g. -28800 for PST).

RootGroup.description: Primary group of the root user. Typically "root" on Linux, "wheel" on macOS/BSD.

name.description: Group name for root's primary GID.

Shells.description: Valid login shells installed on the host, parsed from /etc/shells.

paths.description: Absolute paths to available login shells (e.g. ["/bin/bash", "/bin/zsh", "/usr/bin/fish"]).

FIPS.description: FIPS 140-2/140-3 mode detection: kernel-level flag and user-space crypto policy.

enabled.description: True when the kernel booted with fips=1 (FIPS 140-2 mode).

FIPSPolicy.description: User-space crypto policy state from /etc/crypto-policies/config (RHEL 8+, Fedora 30+).

fips_effective.description: True when the policy name starts with "FIPS", indicating FIPS 140-3 mode is effectively active.

OSRelease.description: Fields parsed from /etc/os-release (os-release(5) format). Linux only.

id_like.description: Related OS identifiers (e.g. ["debian"] for Ubuntu, ["rhel", "fedora"] for CentOS).

pretty_name.description: Pretty OS name with version (e.g. "Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (Noble Numbat)").

extra.description: Any os-release keys not explicitly parsed above.

LSB.description: Linux Standard Base identification fields from `lsb_release -a`.

Init.description: Init system detection: identifies the process manager running as PID 1.

Shard.description: Deterministic shard seed derived from stable host identity (machine name + DMI serial + DMI UUID, MD5-hashed). Same host always maps to the same bucket.

seed.description: Shard seed integer (0 to 2^28-1). First 7 hex chars of the MD5 hash interpreted as base-16.

path.description: Absolute filesystem path to the package manager binary.

GCE.description: Google Compute Engine instance metadata from the GCE metadata server.

tags.description: Network tags applied to the instance.

preemptible.description: True if this is a preemptible (Spot) instance.

automatic_restart.description: Whether the instance restarts automatically on host failures.

licenses.description: GCP license IDs attached to the instance.

attributes.description: Instance-level metadata key-value pairs (SSH keys, startup scripts, etc.).

service_accounts.description: GCP service accounts attached to the instance.

virtual_clock_drift_token.description: Opaque drift token from the virtualClock subtree. Used to detect VM migration.

remaining_cpu_time.description: Remaining CPU time in seconds before preemption for Spot VMs. -1 on standard instances.

partner_attributes.description: Opaque key-value pairs set by GCE partner images.

ip_aliases.description: Alias IP ranges assigned to this NIC.

GCEAccessConfig.description: External access configuration on a GCE NIC (public IP + NAT type).

encrypted.description: True if the disk is encrypted.

GCEServiceAccount.description: One GCP service account attached to the instance.

key.description: Service account key (usually "default" or the SA email).

scopes.description: OAuth scopes granted to this service account.

EC2.description: AWS EC2 instance metadata from IMDS (IMDSv2 with IMDSv1 fallback).

ami_id.description: AMI ID the instance was launched from.

ami_launch_index.description: Launch index when multiple instances started from same request.

ami_manifest_path.description: S3 path to the AMI manifest (instance-store AMIs only).

public_hostname.description: Public DNS hostname (empty when no public IP).

public_ipv4.description: Public IPv4 address (empty in VPC without EIP).

network_interfaces.description: Per-ENI metadata keyed by MAC address.

public_keys.description: SSH public keys in OpenSSH format.

block_device_mapping.description: Map of virtual disk name (ami, root, ebs0) to device path (/dev/sda1).

api_version.description: IMDS API version negotiated for this collection.

user_data.description: Raw user-data blob. Base64-encoded when binary, plaintext when UTF-8.

local_hostname.description: Internal DNS hostname for this ENI.

local_ipv4s.description: Private IPv4 addresses on this ENI.

network_card_index.description: Network card index for multi-card instances.

owner_id.description: AWS account ID that owns this ENI.

EC2IAMInfo.description: IAM instance profile info (credentials are excluded).

last_updated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last credential refresh.

Azure.description: Azure instance metadata from the IMDS at 169.254.169.254.

is_host_compatibility_layer_vm.description: True if running in Azure's host compatibility layer.

public_keys.description: SSH public keys from the VM profile.

interfaces.description: Network interfaces keyed by MAC address.

public_ipv4.description: All public IPv4 addresses across all interfaces.

local_ipv4.description: All private IPv4 addresses across all interfaces.

public_ipv6.description: All public IPv6 addresses across all interfaces.

local_ipv6.description: All private IPv6 addresses across all interfaces.

AzureDisk.description: One Azure OS or data disk.

path.description: Path where the key is deployed.

AzureExtendedLocation.description: Non-standard Azure placement (edge zone, Arc, Azure Local).

anchor_ipv4.description: Anchor IPv4 for floating/reserved IP routing.

OCI.description: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure instance metadata from OCI IMDS.

volume_attachments.description: Attached volumes keyed by attachment OCID.

Alibaba.description: Alibaba Cloud ECS instance metadata from the IMDS at 100.100.100.200.

ram_role_name.description: RAM role name attached to the instance.

disks.description: Attached disks keyed by serial number.

Linode.description: Linode instance metadata. Detected via apt sources or domain heuristic; IPs come from host network interfaces.

OpenStack.description: OpenStack (Nova) instance metadata from the EC2-compatible metadata service and Nova's meta_data.json.

public_keys.description: SSH public keys keyed by name.

Scaleway.description: Scaleway instance metadata from the metadata server at 169.254.42.42.

public_ip_dynamic.description: True if the public IP is dynamically assigned.

DMI.description: SMBIOS/DMI data: BIOS, baseboard, chassis, and product identity from /sys/class/dmi/id/ via ghw.

DMIProduct.description: System identity (DMI type 1). Product.name is the primary signal cloud collectors use.

name.description: System product name (e.g. "Google Compute Engine", "Amazon EC2").

GPU.description: Graphics/compute cards. Linux: from ghw/gpu via /sys/class/drm. macOS: from system_profiler SPDisplaysDataType.

cards.description: List of detected GPU/graphics devices. Empty on headless servers or containers without /sys/class/drm.

model.description: GPU model (e.g. "Apple M1 Pro", "GP107 [GeForce GTX 1050 Ti]").

address.description: PCI address on Linux, bus descriptor on macOS.

cores.description: GPU core count (macOS integrated Apple GPUs only).

PCI.description: PCI devices enumerated from /sys/bus/pci/devices via ghw. Linux only.

driver.description: Kernel driver bound to this device.

SCSI.description: SCSI devices from lsscsi output. Linux only.

devices.description: SCSI devices keyed by SCSI address (e.g. "0:0:0:0").

Hardware.description: macOS-specific hardware facts from system_profiler (SPHardwareDataType, SPStorageDataType, SPPowerDataType). Empty on Linux.

HardwareStorage.description: One macOS storage volume from SPStorageDataType.

HardwareCharger.description: MacOS AC charger info from SPPowerDataType.
